rulings.Case Title: SINDY CRISOSTOMO-RIVERA VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing (On Petition For Writ Of Mandate)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 15. PETITIONER SINDY CRISOSTOMO-RIVERA'S Hearing on Petition for Writ of Mandate. Petitioner Sindy Crisostomo-Rivera's Petition for Writ of Mandate, or in the alternative Petition for Writ of Prohibition and Attorney's Fees and Costs, is denied. The complete tentative ruling has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BRITT ET AL VS. ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING AND ENGINEERING, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT ROLLAMATIC ROOFS, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Specially appearing party Rollamatic Roofs, Inc. has moved to quash service of summons on it based on an alleged unreasonable delay in identifying it as a Doe defendant in this ongoing case. The court denies the motion. Leave to amend to name a previously-unknown Doe defendant may be denied, and a motion to quash granted, where the plaintiff has unreasonably delayed the amendment and where prejudice is shown. The court previously continued the hearing and ordered Plaintiffs to supply facts about whether destructive testing of the construction in dispute had occurred. Plaintiffs have done so. The court now finds that there is no unreasonable delay and no prejudice in light of Plaintiffs' showing that destructive testing has not occurred, that discovery is ongoing, and that no trial date has been set. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: FABIAN PHILLIP COOKIE JR VS. BOUTIQUE AIR, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 5. DEFENDANT BOUTIQUE AIR, INC., OPEN TRIP INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Defendant Boutique Air Inc. moves for judgment on the pleadings on all of plaintiff Fabian Philip Cooke Jr.'s causes of action. The court accepts all facts pleaded as true, drawing reasonable inferences in favor of the complaint, but does not accept legal conclusions. Accordingly, the court denies the motion for judgment on the first through third causes of action. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted as to causes of action four through ten and denied as to causes of action one through three and eleven. Leave to amend is denied. rulings.Case Title: FANX, INC. VS. JAMES DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions And Production
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANTS JAMES DAVIS, SAMANTHA DAVIS DOOLITTLE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions And Production. Moving parties James Davis and Samantha Davis Doolittle ("Davises") seek an order compelling responding party FanX, Inc. to respond to its Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admission. The moving papers demonstrate that the Davises served this discovery on FanX, FanX did not timely respond, and FanX has not yet responded. By failing to respond, FanX has waived its objections to the requests. (CCP 2031.300(a); 2033.280(a).) FanX opposes this relief on the grounds that this court's order of November 2, 2023, denying arbitration was erroneous. FanX avers that it has sought writ review of the November 2 order, and in the interim it acted with substantial justification in not participating in discovery. This argument is misplaced. Regardless of a party's belief in the correctness of an order, it must follow the order unless the party has sought and obtained a stay of the litigation from the trial court or has obtained a stay of the litigation or reversal of the order from the appellate court. The court orders as follows: the matters specified in the Davises' Requests for Admission, Set One, are deemed admitted by FanX. (CCP 2033.230(b), (c).) FanX is ordered to provide code-compliant responses to the Requests for Production, without objections, within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. FanX must complete its document production within 45 days of notice of this order. No objections may be asserted. The court shall impose monetary sanctions on a party who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel responses to discovery unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of sanctions unjust. (CCP 2031.300(c).) It is mandatory that the court award sanctions where a party fails to respond to requests for admissions. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court does not find that FanX acted with substantial justification here and imposes the requested amount. FanX shall pay $1860 to the Davises in sanctions within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: GLEB FINKELMAN VS. CLEAN SOLAR, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Clean Solar
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 13. PLAINTIFF GLEB FINKELMAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Clean Solar. Plaintiff moves to compel further responses, and for sanctions, from defendant Clean Solar, Inc. Plaintiff's moving papers demonstrated that Clean Solar did not provide code-compliant responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, although it did produce documents. In particular, Clean Solar's responses did not contain a representation of compliance (CCP 2031.210(a)(1)) or a statement of objection and the documents falling within a category to which objection was made (CCP 2031.240(b)(1)). Clean Solar now contends the motion is moot because it provided code-compliant responses after Plaintiff filed his motion. (McHugh Dec. para. 6 & Ex. A.) The court finds Clean Solar has now provided compliant responses but its failure to do so in meet and confer (and its failure to extend Plaintiff's time to file a motion to compel) necessitated this motion. The court does not find that Clean Solar acted with substantial justification and awards sanctions of $750 payable to Plaintiff by Clean Solar within 10 court days of entry of this order. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390, 405 [sanctions provision applies even where party serves responses after motion is filed].) The discovery statutes are supposed to be self-executing and a party should not be required to bring a motion to obtain a compliant response. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YESENIA GONZALEZ VS. KELLY SAU MING YUENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 14. DEFENDANT KELLY YEUNG'S Motion To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Kelly Sau Ming Yeung's unopposed discovery motion is granted as stated herein. The moving papers establish that defendant served Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; Requests for Production of Documents, Set One; and Requests for Admission, Set One on Plaintiff Yesenia Gonzalez. (Mazzocone Dec. paras. 3-6.) Gonzalez has not responded. (Mazzocone Dec. para. 14.) Gonzalez has waived objections to the discovery requests by failing to timely respond. The court orders Plaintiff to respond, without objections, to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, within 20 calendar days of entry of this order. (See CCP 2030.290(a) [interrogatories]; CCP 2031.3009(a) [requests for production].) Plaintiff shall produce all documents responsive to the RFPs within 30 calendar days of entry of this order. Further, Defendant's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Defendant seeks sanctions. The discovery statutes state that the court shall impose a monetary sanction against a party who misuses the discovery process, including by failing to respond to an authorized method of discovery, unless it finds that the party who does so acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (CCP 2023.010(d); 2030.290(c); 2031.300(c).) Where a party fails to respond to requests for admissions, sanctions are mandatory. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court does not find substantial justification in any event and awards sanctions of $2700 payable by Plaintiff to Defendant. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 20 calendar days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: On the law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 8. rulings.Case Title: BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, VS. TANTALUS BUILDERS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 9. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TANTALUS BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SETH WHITAKER VS. SERVICENOW, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 10. DEFENDANT SERVICENOW, INC.'S Motion To Stay. Defendant Servicenow, Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings pending appeal is granted. The complete tentative ruling has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALISSA MONIQUE TORRES DIAZ ET AL VS. ANKITA KHANNA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 14, 2025, Line 11. DEFENDANT ANKITA KHANNA'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's unopposed demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the ground that the complaint is so uncertain that it does not fairly apprise defendant of what is claimed against her. A demurrer for uncertainty will rarely lie and is strictly construed. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Here, however, the complaint refers to defendant Khanna as the plaintiff at page 5, and it is not clear from the face of the complaint what her role is in the auto accident alleged to have occurred. Plaintiff has leave to amend within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
